todo.text: [Trading] 📝 Draft: prepare the first company analysis lesson for Philippe's followed companies; deliverable: markdown lesson in projects/company-research/ using named tickers from his followed-companies list | Why: Business-analysis learning should start from real companies Philippe actually follows | Proof: Lesson uses a defined company list and focuses on one concrete beginner exercise | Unlocks: First guided company-analysis practice task

[Personal] 📚 Learn: build one beginner Python study sheet on variables, input/output, and simple conditionals; deliverable: markdown lesson in projects/python-learning/ with 5 read-and-predict examples and 3 tiny exercises | Why: Python should be learned like a language: fundamentals first, then reading, guided exercises, and only then practical scripts | Proof: Lesson includes explanations, read-first examples, and beginner exercises Philippe can complete without advanced concepts | Unlocks: Progress toward: Write a simple script with input validation

[Other] 🔧 Fix: identify one repetitive workspace/OpenClaw workflow that wastes time, excluding `openclaw.json` and any live config mutation path; deliverable: markdown spec in projects/automation/ with evidence, proposed automation, and safe input/output boundaries | Why: Reduces repetitive manual work, freeing time for skill-building | Proof: Spec identifies a real workflow bottleneck, stays outside config mutation, and defines safe boundaries clearly | Unlocks: Progress toward: Write a simple script with input validation

[Personal] 📚 Learn: create one focused Japanese study pack from a single beginner theme; deliverable: markdown lesson in projects/language/ with vocab, readings, and 5 short practice prompts | Why: Spaced repetition with focused vocabulary and pattern practice builds usable language skill | Proof: Deck or exercise set has complete entries and is usable for deliberate practice | Unlocks: Unlocks the next stage of reading and basic output practice

inprogress.text: [Career] 🔨 Build: create a daily creative challenge generator page or tool that gives Philippe one finishable same-day assignment; deliverable: working page or tool in projects/creative-challenges/ that automatically chooses the daily category across Blender, After Effects, Unreal Engine, Illustrator, commercial copywriting, and storytelling/novel writing, then shows only the assigned challenge with optional light visual support | Why: Structured daily challenge generation turns broad creative goals into consistent, scoped practice without decision friction | Proof: Tool assigns one different day-sized challenge per day without user category selection and shows only the prompt/challenge for that day | Unlocks: Reusable daily practice system for portfolio, writing, and creative skill growth
